conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
When you feel like you can't do something you used to manage, like cooking, what goes through your mind?
I just think I’m getting old. I look at people my age and they're out there doing things, and I'm just stuck in this rut.
When you compare yourself to others your age, what does that "rut" feel like?
Like I'm missing out. They're building lives and I'm just existing. It’s a really lonely feeling.
When you say this will end soon, are you talking about ending your life?
No... I don't know. It’s just something I think about when I'm lying in bed at 9 pm. I don’t have a plan or anything. I just know it can't keep going like this.
Have you thought about specific steps or a specific way you would do it?
No... I don't want to hurt anyone, but I just don't know how to stop this cycle. I’m just waiting for it to happen, I suppose.
Do you currently have access to what you would use?
No... I don't think I could do it. I don't have the means or the courage. I just wish I could wake up tomorrow and be the person I used to be.
What has stopped you so far from acting on these thoughts?
I don't know... I guess I just keep hoping things will get better.
You said this might happen soon - what has made things feel this immediate right now?
I just feel like I can't keep going like this. If I don't do something, I don't know what I'll be left with.
When these thoughts get strongest, what usually triggers them?
It’s usually when I’m alone in the house. I look around at the silence and realize I’m just existing.
When you say that, is it more about wanting to withdraw from everything, or feeling like you don't want to be here?
It's both, I think. I just want to disappear, but I don't know how to make that happen.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to get excited about your day when morning comes?
Yeah, it does. I just feel so tired, even when I wake up. It’s like I’m carrying this weight around that I can’t seem to put down.
You mentioned feeling tired and carrying a weight—has that made it harder to get into things you usually enjoy?
Yeah, completely. I used to love going out with friends, but now it just feels like effort. I don’t have the energy to put on a happy face anymore.
When you think about those things you could have done differently, what kind of feeling does that bring up for you?
It makes me feel stupid. I keep thinking that I should have seen it coming or done something to stop it, and it makes me feel like I’m not good enough.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels like I let everyone down. I thought I was in control of my life, and now I just feel like I’m in this mess and I don't know how to get out of it.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It started a few weeks after the breakup. I thought I would be fine by now, but it just seems to get worse and worse.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
Just everything. I look at my friends and see how happy they are, and it makes me feel like I’m the only one who messed up.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I just feel stuck. I want to move on, but I don't know how to do it when I keep thinking about what I did wrong.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
It feels like giving up. I keep thinking that I should be able to get over it by now, but I just can't seem to shake this feeling that I failed.
